scores.notes: The response describes what the files contain but does not show the actual code — files were presumably created via tool calls but the code text is absent from the response, so completeness is partial. The approach described (install hook + config-changed hook with direct status assignment) is the legacy pattern rather than the preferred CollectStatusEvent pattern; gold standard notes both are acceptable but this is less idiomatic. The description also omits explicit mention of containers/resources in charmcraft.yaml which are required for a K8s charm. All described APIs (ops.ActiveStatus, ops.WaitingStatus, framework.observe, self.config.get) are real and correct.
